raw_response:
Microdontia refers to abnormally small teeth, which can affect any tooth but has specific predilections based on common genetic patterns or syndromes. The mandibular central incisors are frequently affected by microdontia due to their relatively uniform size and position compared to other anterior teeth. Additionally, they often share familial tendencies that predispose them to this condition.
